verses.text: God tells Moses one plague more, then Pharaoh will thrust them out; Israel borrows jewels, and Moses is great in Egypt. Last blow. The final stroke will break Pharaoh's will.
God tells Moses one plague more, then Pharaoh will thrust them out; Israel borrows jewels, and Moses is great in Egypt. Last blow. The final stroke will break Pharaoh's will.
God tells Moses one plague more, then Pharaoh will thrust them out; Israel borrows jewels, and Moses is great in Egypt. Last blow. The final stroke will break Pharaoh's will.
Moses warns that at midnight every firstborn of Egypt shall die, with 'a great cry.' Midnight death. The worst judgment is announced.
Moses warns that at midnight every firstborn of Egypt shall die, with 'a great cry.' Midnight death. The worst judgment is announced.
Moses warns that at midnight every firstborn of Egypt shall die, with 'a great cry.' Midnight death. The worst judgment is announced.
But against Israel 'not a dog move his tongue'; Egypt's servants shall bow and beg them to go. A difference. God divides between Egypt and His people.
But against Israel 'not a dog move his tongue'; Egypt's servants shall bow and beg them to go. A difference. God divides between Egypt and His people.
Pharaoh will not hearken, that God's wonders be multiplied; his heart is hardened. Hardened. His refusal serves God's larger purpose.
